about:
I’m an "accidental" sales professional, turned 20-year veteran and now lifelong student of the craft. I'm passionate about building diverse and collaborative teams, coaching and developing talent, accelerating sales performance and helping customers innovate the way they work - and of course, having fun while doing it.

Currently, I am leading Commercial Sales at Notion, supporting both expansion and greenfield growth of our fastest growing customer segment. At Notion, we are changing the way companies work. 

Notion 3.0 empowers professionals and teams to instantly access knowledge, automate tasks, and generate high-impact content—all in one place—so you can focus on driving results and growing your business with confidence, efficiency and speed. 

Connect with me to learn more. And always open to networking and trading notes on the ever-changing sales landscape, leadership, AI, change management, podcasts, good reads, NYC restaurants, parenting, dance, or yoga.

experience.description: As our customer base grew, so did our team. When we officially segmented our SMB business, I shifted up-market to lead our Mid-Market and Enterprise customer team.

experience.position: Regional Manager, Relationship Management, Sales Solutions
experience.description: I was a founding member of LinkedIn Sales Solutions, hired to help establish and build the relationship management function in New York. My team supported the launch of LinkedIn's flagship Sales Navigator solution and led relationships with our early adopter small, medium and large enterprise customers to inspire them to embrace the "new" world of social selling.

During my stint as an individual contributor, I realized my passion for coaching and impacting the success of others. For four years, I led the account management function for CEB’s IT and Legal memberships for midsized companies, while supporting 1300+ members worldwide. 

In this role, I worked with my team to shape client service and renewal strategies, helped translate market voice into research and development efforts, led two successful new product launches, and managed complex negotiations with senior-level executives. CEB is best known in the sales community for their work authoring the Challenger Sales Methodology.

As the CEB IT membership network experienced 40% year-over-year growth, the account management team expanded to support it. Having been successful at supporting my own members, I had the opportunity to take on a stretch / hybrid role of individual contributor plus sales management. In this role, I continued to engage and support C-level IT executives and their teams, while coaching, mentoring, and contributing to the overall success of my team.

I pursued my lifelong passion for dance and was honored to serve as team captain for 37 women, giving me a new platform to flex my leadership and team building, mentoring, choreography and performance coaching skills. As an NFL spokesperson, I had the opportunity to give back to the DMV community through participation in 75+ charity and sponsored events, including three Military Appreciation Tours to entertain U.S. Troops stationed in Japan, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar and UAE.

experience.position: Account Director, CEB IT Leadership Council
experience.description: After working with prospective clients for a year, I realized that I was more interested in delivering ongoing customer value and building long-term relationships. In this role, I helped develop the account management function for a new CEB membership program, while managing a portfolio of 130 high-growth IT departments to identify key market trends and drive team utilization of membership website and services.

experience.position: Senior Sales Associate, CEB Marketing Practice
experience.description:
CEB is a leading global member-based advisory firm that supports 90% of the Fortune 500. By leveraging the collective wisdom of functionally-aligned membership networks, CEB identifies and then shares organizational best practices to help address common business challenges – ultimately enabling their members gain operational efficiency, drive professional growth, and transform their business operations. 

In this role, I partnered with an outside sales executive to help identify, qualify, and engage leads within a specific territory to educate them on the benefits of CEB’s sales and marketing products / services and grow the membership.

As a student athlete, I had an opportunity to intern for the athletics department to plan and execute promotions and giveaways at Penn State Sporting Events. I like to think of this as my first sales gig, responsible for driving attendance and fan experience for non-revenue generating sports.

Susan has a unique way of pushing and challenging people. She’s caring and precise; thoughtful and collaborative. She’s also incredibly adept at building teams. Susan's team are balanced, inclusive, supportive, and spirited. The way we cared for each other was truly unique and stood in stark contrast against traditional sales culture. It’s worth noting for much of 2016, Susan essentially held 3 roles. She managed 2 full sales teams and renewed a book of enterprise-level accounts with remarkable finesse and grace. It’s no surprise she was awarded Manager of the Year. I know I’m a better person and professional from having had the opportunity to work for Susan. Please consider yourself fortunate if you have the opportunity to call her your peer, manager, or friend!

headline: J’aide les indépendants et dirigeants à signer des clients grâce à leurs posts LinkedIn, sans pub ni prospection | Accompagnement 1:1 | Système | Outils | Stratégie
about:
Tu veux développer ton business grâce à LinkedIn.
Mais 3 choses te bloquent, les mêmes que TOUT créateur connaît :

1) Tu publies, mais ça ne génère pas assez de clients.
Tu as de l’audience, des likes, mais peu d’opportunités concrètes.

2) Tu perds un temps fou à créer, organiser, publier et analyser.
Ton système est dispersé : une idée ici, un post là, des stats ailleurs.

3) Tu n’as aucune machine.
Chaque post dépend de toi. Rien n’est automatisé. Rien ne tourne quand tu t’arrêtes.

J’ai vécu la même galère :
✖︎ des idées partout
✖︎ des posts jamais terminés
✖︎ des stats que je consultais “quand j’avais le temps”
✖︎ des DM ou leads que j’oubliais complètement

✖︎ et surtout : aucune vision claire de ce qui convertissait réellement.

Et un jour, j’ai compris un truc simple :
Créer du contenu ne suffit pas, il faut un système qui transforme ce contenu en clients.

Alors j’ai construit l’outil que j’aurais rêvé d’avoir depuis le début.

OP Content, c’est ton écosystème Notion + LinkedIn :
✔︎ un espace unique qui réunit tout ton pipeline de contenu
✔︎ toutes tes automatisations LinkedIn
✔︎ et l’IA qui t’assistent à chaque étape

1. Tu centralises tout dans Notion
↳ Stratégie
↳ Idées
↳ Scripts
↳ Carrousels
↳ Images
↳ Posts
↳ Calendrier
↳ Rapports
↳ Graphiques
↳ Recyclage

2. Tu automatises LinkedIn sans effort
↳ Publication automatique
↳ Récupération des stats toutes les heures
↳ Acceptation automatique des demandes de connexion + message d’accueil
↳ Récupération des leads magnet
↳ Envoi d’emails ou messages automatiquement
↳ Suivi d’opportunités

3. Tu actives les agents IA intégrés
↳ Génération d’idées
↳ Optimisation de posts
↳ Recyclage intelligent
↳ Analyse de performance
↳ Recommandations stratégiques
↳ Création de contenus multi-formats

Ce que ça fait pour ton business
✔︎ Tu publies plus vite, plus souvent, avec plus d’impact
✔︎ Tu sais exactement ce qui fonctionne et pourquoi
✔︎ Tu arrêtes de perdre des leads
✔︎ Tu crées une machine d’acquisition automatisée
✔︎ Tu transformes enfin ton audience en clients
✔︎ Tout se fait dans Notion, sans complexité

Tu veux t'équiper d’OP Content ?
C'est ici : https://opeez.notion.site/op-content

headline: I help you build a personal brand that attracts clients & opportunities | Founder @launchanyway | Content Creator & Community Builder
about: Hey, I'm Maria — a personal branding coach and consultant based in Berlin. I teach solopreneurs to build personal brands that attract clients and opportunities. Lots of opportunities. And if there's anything I learned this year about myself, it's that I LOVE TEACHING. Being useful, helping you grow and seeing the spark in my student's eyes. So, I've been teaching hundreds of people through workshops, events, 1:1 sessions, communities...After working as a startup marketer for 4+ years, leading content-first growth and turning LinkedIn into the primary source of leads and revenue, not just vanity metrics, I decided to go all-in as a personal branding consultant and content creator.Since then, I've been lucky to work with brands like Framer, Notion, Tally, Pitch, ATworld, GrowthMentor, LeadShark, 12BRAVE, and many more.If you're a solopreneur or founder who needs clear systems and to make your personal brand drive opportunities, clients and leads for you — you're in the right place, and I would love to help!💜 DM me "LINKEDIN GROWTH" and we can explore how I can support you on your journey!I also created and manage two communities — Solopreneur Community (launchanyway.com) and the Notion for Marketers community (as a Notion Ambassador) in Berlin... because I just love people and learning from each other. You can find lots of free resources on my page and YouTube channel (@hellomarialeden).Other than here you can find me at a calisthenics class, grabbing vegan lunch with friends, or co-working at one of my favorite Berlin cafes.

education.fieldOfStudy: Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences – Musicology (Major) and Russian (Minor)
education.description:
I hold a Bachelor's degree in Musicology with a focus on Music Psychology and a minor in Russian language and literature. 

My thesis examined the topic of Hyperscanning and the phenomenon of interpersonal neural synchronisation (INS) during musical interaction and speech. I also analysed cultural and gender implications of Hyperscanning research and the potential impact of emotional contagion on INS.

During my studies, I also delved into Russian and Polish literature and linguistics, including topics such as the connection between photography and autobiography and the expression of politeness in language. Additionally, I tutored non-native Russian speakers, providing support with presentations, speaking practice, and homework assignments.

Today, I work in Digital Marketing, where my diverse educational background adds a unique perspective and strengthens my ability to connect with a wide range of target audiences.

headline: Recruiting @ Notion Japan (We’re hiring!)
about:
I have over 15 years of experience in Talent Acquisition in Japan and APAC, leading teams of recruiters, sourcers, and coordinators. Before joining Notion, I managed TA teams at Slalom, Slack (Salesforce) and Adobe that hired leadership and IC roles across GTM sales, professional services, engineering, marketing, and G&A roles in Japan and APAC. 

I’ve always been a hands on kind of leader, and I have a track record of leading end-to-end recruitment, primarily through direct sourcing. I enjoy collaborating closely with senior leaders across different teams and regions. I’m also passionate about empowering my team members to become successful at what they do, and helping them achieve their professional and personal goals.

I also have a multicultural background and a passion for languages. I speak fluent English and Japanese, and a little bit of Spanish. I'm also a certified Japanese-English visual media content translator, and a singer and performer with a love for musical theater.

experience.description: Leading a team of recruiters, talent sourcer, and recruiting coordinator.

Our team hires top go-to-market talent in Tokyo, who believe in Notion’s mission and are passionate about their craft. 

Notion helps you build beautiful tools for your life’s work. In today's world of endless apps and tabs, Notion provides one place for teams to get everything done, seamlessly connecting docs, notes, projects, calendar, and email—with AI built in to find answers and automate work. Millions of users, from individuals to large organizations like Toyota, Figma, and OpenAI, love Notion for its flexibility and choose it because it helps them save time and money.

Founded in 2013, Notion has grown to 1,200+ employees across offices in San Francisco, New York, Dublin, London, Munich, Seoul, Tokyo, Sydney, and Singapore. With $500M+ in ARR and a $10B valuation, we're scaling rapidly while maintaining our commitment to flexibility and user-centric design. 

I'm particularly focused on building out our Japan sales and leadership team as we expand our presence in one of our most strategic markets.

We're hiring! notion.so/careers

Led a team of TA Managers hiring for primarily technology consulting roles (e.g. technology strategy consultants, cloud & platform engineers, software engineers, experience strategists, UX/UI designers, data engineers & strategists, martech consultants, etc.) Also managed senior leadership hiring for Business Advisory & Technology Advisory practices.
Joined the Salesforce Japan recruiting leadership team through Slack’s acquisition from October 2021.
Managed a team of recruiters focusing on Customer Success & Services, Customer Experience, Engineering, Marketing, and G&A across Japan and APAC (mainly Japan & Korea since the acquisition)

- Established the direct sourcing methodology and role as the first Talent Scout in Japan
- Concurrently scouted on 15+ critical searches at various phases of the recruiting cycle

- Ensured a steady stream of talent to build a diverse pipeline for ongoing and prospective roles
- Marketed and advertised roles and company brand on external sites, blogs, user groups & social media platforms

receivedRecommendations.description:
I owe a huge part of my success to Anna. 

I first had the pleasure of working with Anna when she joined my team at Adobe as a Talent Scout in 2015, and she excelled in the role. We were soon working in a groove where we quadrupled hiring vs previous years and it was quickly apparent that she could handle considerably more than what was on her JD. She hit all of her targets every quarter, provided great input to improve the team’s efficiency and she was quickly operating as a fully fledged Recruiter despite not having the title. Once that was rectified, she took on all of Sales and Marketing Hiring and I knew that I could instantly forget about anything assigned to her - it was going to get done and it was going to get done right. Quite a boon for any Manager. Not long after my departure from Adobe, she was rightfully promoted into a Leadership role.

Fast forward to December 2019, I’m at Slack running JAPAC Recruiting, I have my first Recruiting HC available in Japan and Anna lets me know she may be looking for her next move. I think to myself, “there’s no way the stars can align twice”, but they do. And Anna goes on to literally crush every role placed in front of her on her way to being top of the Recruiting leaderboard globally.

Even as  Sr. Recruiter on the team, she was always involved with onboarding and enabling the more junior members of the team, mentoring team members and educating our hiring stakeholders and it becomes a no-brainer to promote her into the first JAPAC Manager role at Slack, a role in which she has been outstanding. That’s just what she does.

She is passionate about her job, she has a unique eye for spotting deficiencies in our process and suggesting fixes and has been an incredible mentor to both her team of direct reports, but also to the wider team - she always shares so much of herself with every organization she has ever been a part of. 

I wholeheartedly recommend Anna for any Recruiting Leadership role. Snap her up while you can, and before our stars align for the 3rd time.

She had a way of reframing missteps into learning opportunities that left you feeling supported rather than scrutinized, and that psychological safety made me a better, braver professional. about: I am currently the Global Forward Deployed Engineering Lead at Notion where I am shaping the technical direction of our Professional Services group. I lead a team of FDEs as they design and execute AI transformations and build engineering solutions, support data migrations, and address technical challenges for our customers. Previously, I was a Senior Manager of Technical Consulting at Slack, where I led a team of Technical Architects and leveraged my full-stack software engineering and customer-facing experience to scope and design holistic technical solutions for Slack's customers. My role involved overseeing a team to ensure successful planning, execution, and delivery of consulting projects. I provided technical guidance, support, and fostered a positive and collaborative work environment that allowed my team to excel, while also building strong relationships with clients to understand their needs, scope projects, and allocate resources to maximize both the team's productivity and client satisfaction.Prior to Slack, I contributed to web applications at Capital One through frontend feature development for new partners and modifications to downstream APIs to decision an applicant. During that time, I also completed my Master’s in Computer Science from Georgia Tech, became an AWS Data Community Builder and AWS Re:Invent grant recipient, and began supporting underrepresented communities in STEM through Capital One Coders and Women Who Code.

experience.location: New York, New York, United States

experience.description:
I lead a team of technical architects, overseeing projects across the public sector, financial services, and consumer/business services industries. Responsibilities include customer relationship management, scoping technical consulting engagements such as custom Slack applications, and fostering team growth. 

Contributions include establishing a long-term technical consolidation strategy for Slack instances in collaboration with engineering and product stakeholders, providing expertise in both ETL scripts and M&A activities as a whole, as well as supporting new JavaScript tooling to streamline data movement processes, reduce risk and customer downtime, and improve end-to-end efficiencies.

· Delivered the features for our Bass Pro/Cabela’s partner for Capital One’s core credit card originations application using a React and the Flux design pattern coupled with a microservices architecture
· Lead annual peak season performance testing and disaster recovery exercise for a Customer Data Prefill API for our Sony partner by failing the API over to the West region, identifying dependencies on lower resiliency tiers, creating a contingency plan for failback, and testing for failures in the autoscaling of our EC2 instances
· Designed and maintained alerting and monitoring for all partnerships products on our core originations application
· Migrated a legacy mainframe application to the AWS Cloud, which involved debugging as a result of Java versioning changes, implementing unit tests to onboard the application to a DevOps pipeline, and replacing existing brittle tests

· Contributed to design and implementation of an off-cycle Compliance assessment to quantify associate understanding of compliance requirements and drive directional decisions about learning strategies

projects.title: Recommendation for Mom: mining large-scale e-commerce data
projects.description:
Our primary goal is to learn from the shopping patterns collected from the baby product category through an online C2C platform, and make recommendations to new parents who are lack of experience in purchasing baby product by learning from existing baby product transaction history. The prediction comes as a collaborative filtering algorithm incorporating the time-serial nature of the shopping baskets of parents. In another word, we can learn from other parents what they bought for their babies at the same age, and then educate the new parents about what to keep an eye on.

We conducted association analysis and sequence analysis on the transaction data of 40,000 users over 2 years in Chinese largest e-commerce website and made recommendation for what to buy for baby products based on our data analysis results.

projects.title: Crowd-sourced Financial Support: Kiva lender networks
projects.description: Drawing on the previous literature about the effects of Kiva teams on lending behaviors and outcomes and SNS methods, this study looks at how the lender network of Kiva based on the team membership correlates with the fulfillment of loans and the outcomes of the fulfilled loans. In particular, we are interested in how lender network structures correlates with (1) how soon a loan is funded, as well as (2) whether the loan gets funded by the deadline.
